ddls:
CREATE TABLE "configurations" (
  "configuration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration */,
  "LL_static_lambda1" INTEGER /* Value of lambda1 for the linear programming relaxation */,
  "LL_static_lambda2" INTEGER /* Value of lambda2 for the linear programming relaxation */,
  "LL_static_lda" INTEGER /* Value of lda for the linear discriminant analysis */,
  "LL_static_crossOverBias" REAL /* Value of cross-over bias */,
  "seed" INTEGER /* Seed value for the random number generator */,
  "time" INTEGER /* Time taken to run the algorithm */,
  "bound" INTEGER /* Bound of the optimization problem */,
  "solved" INTEGER /* Whether the configuration was solved successfully */,
  "algorithm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the algorithm type */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the configuration was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the configuration was last updated */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the configuration */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the configuration */,
  "performance_metrics" TEXT /* JSON column to store additional performance metrics */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("configuration_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, admin) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was last updated */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_users" (
  "configuration_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user was associated with the configuration */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the association was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("configuration_id", "user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "algorithm_types" (
  "algorithm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each algorithm */,
  "algorithm_name" TEXT /* Name of the algorithm (e.g., linear programming, nonlinear optimization) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the algorithm */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the algorithm type was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the algorithm type was last updated */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the algorithm type is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("algorithm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_algorithm" (
  "configuration_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration */,
  "algorithm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the algorithm */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the configuration was associated with the algorithm */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the association was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("configuration_id", "algorithm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "configuration_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the metric was recorded */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the metric was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_configuration_id FOREIGN KEY ("configuration_id") REFERENCES configurations ("configuration_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON column to store role permissions */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_role_associations" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the association was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the association was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)
